questions.title: Maximum Sum Subarray of Size K
questions.prompt:
Given an array of integers and a number k, find the maximum sum of any contiguous subarray of size k.

You should avoid recalculating every subarray sum from scratch.
questions.svg_html: <svg viewBox="0 0 760 220" width="100%" height="auto" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg"><rect width="760" height="220" rx="18" fill="rgba(255,255,255,0.68)" stroke="rgba(31,35,40,0.18)"/><text x="24" y="36" font-size="16" font-weight="900" fill="#1f2328">Maximum Sum Subarray of Size K</text><text x="24" y="60" font-size="12" font-weight="700" fill="#5b6570">Slide the window: add next element, remove leftmost element</text><g font-size="14" font-weight="900" fill="#1f2328"><rect x="44" y="94" width="56" height="40" rx="10" fill="rgba(255,255,255,0.92)" stroke="rgba(31,35,40,0.15)"/><text x="72" y="120" text-anchor="middle">2</text><rect x="108" y="94" width="56" height="40" rx="10" fill="rgba(255,255,255,0.92)" stroke="rgba(31,35,40,0.15)"/><text x="136" y="120" text-anchor="middle">1</text><rect x="172" y="94" width="56" height="40" rx="10" fill="rgba(255,159,28,0.28)" stroke="rgba(31,35,40,0.15)"/><text x="200" y="120" text-anchor="middle">5</text><rect x="236" y="94" width="56" height="40" rx="10" fill="rgba(255,159,28,0.28)" stroke="rgba(31,35,40,0.15)"/><text x="264" y="120" text-anchor="middle">1</text><rect x="300" y="94" width="56" height="40" rx="10" fill="rgba(255,159,28,0.28)" stroke="rgba(31,35,40,0.15)"/><text x="328" y="120" text-anchor="middle">3</text><rect x="364" y="94" width="56" height="40" rx="10" fill="rgba(255,255,255,0.92)" stroke="rgba(31,35,40,0.15)"/><text x="392" y="120" text-anchor="middle">2</text></g><text x="460" y="118" font-size="15" font-weight="900" fill="#1f2328">5 + 1 + 3 = 9</text></svg>
hints: First compute the sum of the first k elements.
Then slide the window one step at a time.
Add the new element entering the window.
Subtract the old element leaving the window.
questions.starter_code:
def max_sum_subarray(arr, k):
    # TODO
    return 0

print(max_sum_subarray([2,1,5,1,3,2], 3))
print(max_sum_subarray([2,3,4,1,5], 2))
questions.solution_code:
def max_sum_subarray(arr, k):
    if k <= 0 or k > len(arr):
        return 0

    window_sum = sum(arr[:k])
    max_sum = window_sum

    for i in range(k, len(arr)):
        window_sum += arr[i]
        window_sum -= arr[i - k]
        max_sum = max(max_sum, window_sum)

    return max_sum

print(max_sum_subarray([2,1,5,1,3,2], 3))
print(max_sum_subarray([2,3,4,1,5], 2))

questions.title: Longest Substring Without Repeating Characters
questions.prompt:
Given a string, return the length of the longest substring without repeating characters.

Use a moving window and shrink it whenever a duplicate appears.
questions.svg_html: <svg viewBox="0 0 760 220" width="100%" height="auto" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg"><rect width="760" height="220" rx="18" fill="rgba(255,255,255,0.68)" stroke="rgba(31,35,40,0.18)"/><text x="24" y="36" font-size="16" font-weight="900" fill="#1f2328">Longest Unique Substring</text><text x="24" y="60" font-size="12" font-weight="700" fill="#5b6570">Expand right, shrink left when duplicate appears</text><g font-size="14" font-weight="900" fill="#1f2328"><rect x="48" y="98" width="52" height="38" rx="10" fill="rgba(255,159,28,0.28)"/><text x="74" y="123" text-anchor="middle">a</text><rect x="108" y="98" width="52" height="38" rx="10" fill="rgba(255,159,28,0.28)"/><text x="134" y="123" text-anchor="middle">b</text><rect x="168" y="98" width="52" height="38" rx="10" fill="rgba(255,159,28,0.28)"/><text x="194" y="123" text-anchor="middle">c</text><rect x="228" y="98" width="52" height="38" rx="10" fill="rgba(255,255,255,0.9)" stroke="rgba(31,35,40,0.15)"/><text x="254" y="123" text-anchor="middle">a</text></g><text x="48" y="170" font-size="14" font-weight="900" fill="#1f2328">Current best window = "abc"</text></svg>
hints: Use a set to track characters in the current window.
Move the right pointer forward.
If duplicate found, move left pointer until duplicate is removed.
Track the maximum window length.
questions.solution_code:
def longest_unique_substring(s):
    char_set = set()
    left = 0
    max_len = 0

    for right in range(len(s)):
        while s[right] in char_set:
            char_set.remove(s[left])
            left += 1

        char_set.add(s[right])
        max_len = max(max_len, right - left + 1)

    return max_len

print(longest_unique_substring("abcabcbb"))
print(longest_unique_substring("bbbbb"))

questions.title: Minimum Window Substring
questions.prompt:
Given two strings s and t, return the smallest substring of s containing all characters of t.

If no such substring exists, return an empty string.

questions.title: Count Subarrays with Sum K
questions.prompt:
Given an array of integers and a value k, return the number of subarrays whose sum equals k.

This is often solved using prefix sums plus a hash map.
questions.svg_html: <svg viewBox="0 0 760 230" width="100%" height="auto" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg"><rect width="760" height="230" rx="18" fill="rgba(255,255,255,0.68)" stroke="rgba(31,35,40,0.18)"/><text x="24" y="36" font-size="16" font-weight="900" fill="#1f2328">Count Subarrays with Sum K</text><text x="24" y="60" font-size="12" font-weight="700" fill="#5b6570">Use prefix_sum and count how often prefix_sum - k has appeared</text><g font-size="14" font-weight="900" fill="#1f2328"><rect x="48" y="96" width="54" height="38" rx="10" fill="rgba(255,159,28,0.24)"/><text x="75" y="121" text-anchor="middle">1</text><rect x="112" y="96" width="54" height="38" rx="10" fill="rgba(255,159,28,0.24)"/><text x="139" y="121" text-anchor="middle">1</text><rect x="176" y="96" width="54" height="38" rx="10" fill="rgba(255,159,28,0.24)"/><text x="203" y="121" text-anchor="middle">1</text></g><text x="48" y="170" font-size="15" font-weight="900" fill="#1f2328">Answer = 2</text></svg>
hints: Maintain a running prefix sum.
If prefix_sum - k has appeared before, add its frequency to the answer.
Use a dictionary to store prefix sum counts.
Initialize dictionary with {0: 1}.
questions.starter_code:
def count_subarrays_sum_k(nums, k):
    # TODO
    return 0

print(count_subarrays_sum_k([1,1,1], 2))
print(count_subarrays_sum_k([1,2,3], 3))
questions.solution_code:
def count_subarrays_sum_k(nums, k):
    prefix_count = {0: 1}
    prefix_sum = 0
    answer = 0

    for num in nums:
        prefix_sum += num
        answer += prefix_count.get(prefix_sum - k, 0)
        prefix_count[prefix_sum] = prefix_count.get(prefix_sum, 0) + 1

    return answer

print(count_subarrays_sum_k([1,1,1], 2))
print(count_subarrays_sum_k([1,2,3], 3))

questions.title: Longest Non-Decreasing Contiguous Sequence
questions.prompt:
Given an array of integers, return the length of the longest contiguous non-decreasing sequence.

A sequence is non-decreasing if every next element is greater than or equal to the previous one.

This is not a general subsequence problem. The elements must remain contiguous.
questions.svg_html: <svg viewBox="0 0 760 230" width="100%" height="auto" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg"><rect width="760" height="230" rx="18" fill="rgba(255,255,255,0.68)" stroke="rgba(31,35,40,0.18)"/><text x="24" y="36" font-size="16" font-weight="900" fill="#1f2328">Longest Non-Decreasing Contiguous Sequence</text><text x="24" y="60" font-size="12" font-weight="700" fill="#5b6570">Keep extending while current element >= previous element</text><g font-size="14" font-weight="900" fill="#1f2328"><rect x="40" y="98" width="56" height="38" rx="10" fill="rgba(255,255,255,0.92)" stroke="rgba(31,35,40,0.15)"/><text x="68" y="123" text-anchor="middle">1</text><rect x="104" y="98" width="56" height="38" rx="10" fill="rgba(255,159,28,0.28)"/><text x="132" y="123" text-anchor="middle">3</text><rect x="168" y="98" width="56" height="38" rx="10" fill="rgba(255,159,28,0.28)"/><text x="196" y="123" text-anchor="middle">4</text><rect x="232" y="98" width="56" height="38" rx="10" fill="rgba(255,159,28,0.28)"/><text x="260" y="123" text-anchor="middle">4</text><rect x="296" y="98" width="56" height="38" rx="10" fill="rgba(255,159,28,0.28)"/><text x="324" y="123" text-anchor="middle">5</text></g><text x="40" y="176" font-size="15" font-weight="900" fill="#1f2328">Best contiguous length = 5</text></svg>
hints: This is a contiguous sequence problem, not LIS.
Track the current streak length.
If arr[i] >= arr[i-1], extend the streak.
Otherwise reset the streak to 1.
